steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The crucial observation is that the fine mudstone—which dominates the sediment and is deposited in low-flow, settling conditions—shows signs of cracking due to episodic desiccation. This directly indicates that the water body itself periodically dried out, not just the surrounding environment or inflowing streams.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content: A captures the full pattern: a prolonged lake that “occasionally experienced drying,” explaining the mudstone cracks, and “periods in which one or more streams were present,” explaining the coarse grain intervals. B contradicts the evidence by claiming the lake never dried, C limits the lake to a brief period conflicting with mudstone's dominance, and D denies the lake existed altogether. That matches choice A — A is correct.
